details.text: Derivative: مَهْدًا – A place of comfort, cradle or smooth flooring.
Derivative: أَنشَرْنَا from أَنْشَرَ which means to raise up. Here it means to revive, resurrect or bring the land to life through water.
Derivative: مُقْرِنِينَ from أَقْرَنَ which means to have power and control over something.
Derivative: تَسْتَوُوا from اِسْتَوَى which means to set yourself upright and even referring here to mounting a riding animal and sitting firmly and evenly upon it.

Derivative: جُزْءًا means a part or portion (of something). Here it is being used to refer to the attribution of offspring to Allah. جُزْءًا means something that is attributed to Him as if it is a part of Him.
Derivative: كَظِيمٌ means to swallow or suppress one’s rage or grief.

Derivative: يَخْرُصُونَ from خَرَصَ which means to make wild guesses or to make uninformed conjectures. خَرَّاص is the person who makes statements or forms conclusions based on pure conjecture and guessing without having any proof to back them up i.e., to make something up whilst making it sound compelling.
Derivative: مُسْتَمْسِكُونَ from اِسْتَمْسَكَ – اِسْتِمْسَاك which means to hold onto tightly or firmly.
Derivative: أُمَّةٍ refers to a nation and legacy. Here it implies a direction, path and singular focus.
Derivative: مُتْرَفُو which is مُتْرَفُونَ are those who are blinded and corrupted by their great wealth and luxury.
Derivative: مُّقْتَدُونَ from اِقْتَدَى refers to those who are committed to following and proceeding along the same path, model or example as others. Those who emulate others.
Derivative: الْمُكَذِّبِينَ – المُكَذِّبُ from كَذَّبَ is someone who calls something else a lie and denies it.

Derivative: بَرَاءٌ means to be free from something and a declaration of not having any association with something or a renunciation of it. It is more powerful than بَريءٌ. بَرَاءٌ can be used with the singular, dual and plural without change.

Derivative: جَعَلْنَا – From جَعَلَ which means to make. In Arabic جَعَلَ (to make) has a different meaning to خَلَقَ which is to create. جَعَلَ (Ja’ala) means to make in respect to something that is already there and to transform it or its use or function whilst خَلَق means to make something from nothing that did not previously exist.
Derivative: مَعَارِجَ means levels by which you ascend higher. It is used in Arabic to refer to stairways.
Derivative: لَمَّا – In this verse لَمَّا carries the unique meaning of إِلَّا (except or apart from) instead of its usual meaning of “when” or “not” when used before the present tense verb.
Derivative: زُخْرُفًا is something that resembles gold in terms of being shiny and glittery. It refers to the shiny materials which are used to adorn. In this case to adorn the house.

Derivative: يَعْشُ from عَشَا – يَعْشُو which means to ignore, not pay attention to or to turn away from something. The original meaning of عَشَا is to have weak eyesight whereby he is unable to discern something. Consequently, he ignores it and doesn’t pay attention to it.

Derivative: الْمَشْرِقَيْنِ – It literally means the “Two places where the sun rises” and implies an impossible distance i.e., something so far that it cannot be reached. As such, it means two impossible distances to emphasis the greatness of the distance. The majority view however is that الْمَشْرِقَيْنِ "The two places of sunrise or two Easts" refers to the "Place of sunrise (or the East) and the place of sunset (or the West)” (Al-Mashriq (المَشْرِق) and Al-Maghrib (المَغْرِب)). This is because In the Arabic language when there are pairs of something they can both be referred to by using the dual form of one of them. As such the distance between الْمَشْرِقَيْنِ is an expression meaning the distance between the East and the West.
- أُمِّ الْكِتَابِ (Umm il-Kitaab) literally means “The mother of the Book” referring to اللَّوْحُ المَحْفُوظ (The preserved tablet).
Derivative: مُّقْتَدِرُونَ – مُقْتَدِر refers to a greater level of control, capability and power than قَدِير. مُقْتَدِر is someone who has absolute control over something.
Derivative: صَفْحًا from صَفَحَ which means to turn away, to snub, cast aside or disregard something or somebody.
Derivative: نَضْرِبُ from ضَرَبَ means to strike. When followed by عَن it means to turn away from, abandon, leave or neglect something.
- ضَربَ عَن ... صَفْحاً when used together صَفْحًا is used to emphasise ضَربَ عَن i.e., Should we just completely cast aside giving you the Dhikr (reminder) because …
Derivative: يَنكُثُونَ from نَكَثَ which means to go back on an agreement or one’s word. To violate and break the agreement.

Derivative: اسْتَخَفَّ means to attempt to make people dumb, make light of someone and take away their ability to think. It means to attempt to befool and mislead by playing on people’s ignorance – to attempt to sway by propaganda.
Derivative: سَلَفًا means something that is behind, in the past or has preceded. Here it refers to Fir’awn and his people and means that “We made them a precedent from the past”.
Derivative: يَصِدُّونَ means to raise their voices with laughing and mockery – to jeer.
Derivative: يَخْلُفُونَ from خَلَفَ which means to come one after another or succeed one another.
Derivative: عِلْمٌ means knowledge and here it means that ‘Isa (as) will be a “knowledge of” (عِلْم) the coming of the final hour i.e., a known sign.
Derivative: الْأَخِلَّاءُ means the closets friends and is the plural of خَلِيل which is from the words indicating to the closest forms of friendship.

Derivative: تُحْبَرُونَ comes from the word حُبورٌ which means extreme happiness and joy – a level of happiness where nothing can cause you upset. تُحْبَرُونَ is in the passive case (مَجْهول) which indicates that it is a happiness that has been instilled into you.
Derivative: صِحَافٍ are large trays or plates (for food). It is the plural of صَحْفَةٌ which is a large wide plate, the type which a whole family can eat from.

Derivative: تَلَذُّ from لَذَ – لَذِيذٌ which means to be delicious and usually refers to the delight and satisfaction of the tongue. Here it is connected to the eyes i.e., that which pleases and delights them.
Derivative: يُفَتَّرُ from فَتَّرَ which means to give a فَتْرَةٌ which is a period of time, a break or cool off period i.e., the punishment of hell will not abate or be lightened for them.
Derivative: مُبْلِسُونَ in a state of complete hopelessness or abject despair.
Derivative: مَّاكِثُونَ from مَكَثَ which means to remain and linger in a place. مَّاكِثُونَ means “Those who linger and remain”.

Derivative: أَبْرَمُوا from أَبْرَمَ which in origin was used to mean the twisting of rope together to make a strong knot and to make it hold fast. Here it refers to devising a plot or scheme that is precise, solid and strong.
Derivative: بَطْشًا from بَطَشَ which means to grab hold with force, to punish, act violently and ruthlessly.

Derivative: يَخُوضُوا from خَاضَ – خَوْضٌ which means to plunge or delve into something. Here it refers to engaging or delving in false speech and nonsense i.e., to waste time and not be serious in discussion.
Derivative: السَّمَاءِ means the sky but is also general and undefined in terms of referring to that which is above.

Derivative: تَبَارَكَ means to be full of بَرَكَةٌ (blessing) and the power to dispose of بَرَكَةٌ (Barakah). There are two matters related to the word بَرَكَةٌ. The first is what it implies in terms of the increase in goodness (Al-Khair) and more goodness coming than is expected. The second is the extraction of Al-Khair (goodness) and to bring good out of something that has the potential inside. Like the seed cannot produce its goodness until water has been provided to it and in doing so becomes “Mubaarak” water. تَبَارَكَ can be understood to mean: “And how full of Barakah and the power of blessing is He who …”.
Derivative: قِيلِ is the Masdar (مَصْدَر) of قالَ like قَوْلٌ and قالٌ. It means: “Statement” or “That which is said”.
